feature_description: Update the existing HistoryTable component to fetch run data from GET /api/runs on mount using useState and useEffect, replacing the prior synchronous getRunRecords() call. When the response array is empty (including when CODEFORGE_PROJECT_DIR is unset on the server, which causes the API to return an empty array), the component displays a 'No runs found.' empty-state message instead of a data table.

in_scope: Replacing the synchronous getRunRecords() call in HistoryTable with a fetch('/api/runs') call on mount.
Rendering one row per RunSummary object returned by the API when the array is non-empty.
Displaying a 'No runs found.' message when the fetched array is empty.
Component-level tests that mock the fetch call (via vi.fn() or msw) to exercise the populated and empty-array paths.
explicitly_out_of_scope: Changes to GET /api/runs or the RunSummary type — those are already implemented and tested.
A visible loading indicator while the fetch is in-flight.
Explicit fetch error handling or an error state UI — network failures are not addressed by this feature.
Changes to any module other than HistoryTable.
Removing getRunRecords from the RunData module — it may remain exported even if HistoryTable no longer consumes it.

acceptance_criteria.description: On mount, HistoryTable calls fetch('/api/runs') exactly once and, when the response resolves with a non-empty RunSummary array, renders one table row per RunSummary entry using the fetched data (not data from getRunRecords()).

When fetch('/api/runs') resolves with an empty array, HistoryTable renders the text 'No runs found.' and no data rows. This covers both an explicitly empty API response and the case where CODEFORGE_PROJECT_DIR is unset on the server (which causes the API to return []).

relationships: Returned as an array by GET /api/runs (existing, tested interface from 'Run Summaries Reader and API Route' feature).
Consumed by HistoryTable to populate table rows.

changes_from_prior.description: HistoryTable drops its dependency on RunData/getRunRecords and instead fetches data asynchronously from GET /api/runs on mount. The RunData module and getRunRecords interface are unchanged and remain available to other consumers.
interface_changes: HistoryTable no longer consumes getRunRecords or getStatusConfig from RunData. Its own data source is now the /api/runs HTTP endpoint.
